element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Keyboard or digital joystick support with throttle/steering assistance available
Optional driving help feature for track layout learning
Optimal gear suggestions, manual override available
Prevents wheel spin; compensates for digital controller limitations
Smoothens steering inputs for digital controllers

element_introduction_order.element: Track-specific racing lines and braking points
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learned through repeated track runs; requires understanding of realistic physics
Grip degrades throughout race; qualifying tires lose effectiveness quickly
Gear ratios, tire compounds, wing settings affect handling
Special qualifying tires; extremely limited grip for rapid pace development
Practice, qualifying, and race in single session
Full circuit variety requiring diverse driving adaptations

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-release based on track layout; high-speed straights provide relief, technical corners create tension. Tire wear introduces progressive tension throughout race duration.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Driving assistance levels available as toggles; optional car tuning access

difficulty_curve.early_game: Arcade-like with all driving assists enabled; ideal line visible, automatic braking and throttle control
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate: selective assists disabled; player must manage tire wear across multi-lap sequences
difficulty_curve.late_game: Simulation-level: no assists; full physics engagement required; realistic car handling dependent on setup tuning
notable_spikes: Transition from arcade to sim difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Progressive removal of driving assists creates graduated difficulty. Players can start with arcade controls and visual guides, advancing to full simulation physics without restarting.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any single circuit playable from tutorial to sim mode

notable_level_patterns.description: Three-phase session format (practice, qualifying, race) mirrors real Formula One weekend pacing. Qualifying introduces pressure with consumable tire resources.
Mechanic that increases tension over race duration. Qualifying tires degrade within 2 laps; race tires decay throughout full race distance.
16 distinct circuits with accurately modeled layouts. Player recognition of real-world geography creates spatial learning curve; ideal lines differ per track.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Full season spanning all 16 official 1991 F1 circuits
